PR: fix(example): use availWidth/availHeight for SetResolution clamping (follow-up to #1200)
https://github.com/openwpm/OpenWPM/pull/1202

## Unresolved Copilot review threads

- `custom_command.py:57`

  `execute_script` returns JSON-serialized JS numbers, which may come back as `float` (and could also be `Any`), but the helper is annotated as returning `tuple[int, int]`. Casting to `int` here keeps the runtime behavior consistent with the type hint and with the rest of the example (which treats these as pixel integers).

- `custom_command.py:50`

  Docstring references ``screen.availWidth``/``availHeight``; adding `screen.` to `availHeight` avoids ambiguity and matches the actual JS being executed.

---

VERIFY each claim before acting — Copilot is fallible (it once flagged a valid os.getenv(key, default=...) as a bug). If real -> fix + resolve the thread. If wrong -> post a reasoned rebuttal + resolve. Builder -> the-hater -> done; never blindly apply.
